problem: Tool re-execution on task retry has no idempotency guard — duplicate payments, emails, trades possible. When a CrewAI task fails and is retried — via max_retry_limit, exception handling, or external re-trigger — any @tool decorated function that already executed runs again. There's no mechanism to detect that a specific tool call already completed.
memory_hint:
This is a known recurring issue. The temporary fix is: Tool re-execution on task retry has no idempotency guard — duplicate payments, emails, trades possible. A retry of the same logical tool call should return the original result without re-executing the. This works for now but you will need to apply it again when the issue returns. The root cause is 1. Create a CrewAI agent with a @tool that calls an external API (payment, email, etc.)
2. Have the tool execute successfully
3. Simulate a failure af.
